title: Good
description: A purchasable item / service / experience offered by a vendor institution. Per P1 substrate-design-locked.md D11 + D17 + F1=A (separate `goods` table). Loaded into goods table via alembic migration; queried by vendor_selection activity per char per tick (D17 score formula); price mutated by price_adjustment activity at end-of-world-week (D16' v2 formula: clip(old × (1 + 0.10 × utilization_pressure), max(cost_basis × 1.30, old × 0.90), old × 1.10)). Codex B6 fix: table-level GRANT to polis_app required. Codex B9 fix: floor (cost_basis × 1.30) and ceiling (old × 1.10) must respect floor ≤ ceiling invariant. Codex #14 fix: PK is composite (world_id, id) not just (id) so canonical IDs reuse across worlds.

id.description: Slug. Unique within a world (composite PK with world_id per Codex #14). Examples: cafe_meal, pub_dinner, gym_session, match_ticket.

world_id.description: FK to worlds.id. RLS scopes reads by current_setting('polis.world_id') per polis_extensions.sql pattern. Codex #13 fix: CHECK constraint enforces goods.world_id = (SELECT world_id FROM institutions WHERE id = goods.institution_id).

institution_id.description: FK to institutions.id. The vendor party that receives Coin when a char purchases this good. Per P1 D12 institutions-as-employers — vendors are institutions too. Codex #13: same-world constraint enforced via CHECK or trigger.

price_coin.description: Current price in Coin. Mutated by price_adjustment activity at end-of-world-week per D16' v2 formula. INITIAL value set at content authoring time.

cost_basis_coin.description: Per P1 D16' + P0.5 v2 cost_basis × 1.30 floor: vendor's input cost. Price can't go below cost_basis × 1.30 (minimum margin). Default heuristic for content authoring per Codex N10: cost_basis_coin = round(price_coin × 0.7).

need_axis_reductions.description: Per P1 D17 v2 fix (positive magnitudes; Codex B11 direction lock): mapping of needs-axis-name → POSITIVE magnitude by which buying this good REDUCES that axis (lowers HIGH-need toward 0-met). Negative magnitudes (e.g., match_ticket fatigue: -5 means buying tires you SLIGHTLY) are allowed as side-effects but D17 score formula only counts POSITIVE reductions in its weighted_magnitude calculation. Axes MUST match world.config.yaml.needs_axes; lint rule (per P1 lint expansion #2) rejects axis names hardcoded in app code.

district.description: FK to districts.id (NULLABLE — null for district-agnostic goods like transit_weekly). When set, vendor_selection activity filters goods to char's district + district-agnostic via composite index ix_goods_world_district_category. Per P1 D15 pre-wired starter districts.

vendor_category.description: Per P1 D11 v2 schema: substrate-level vendor grouping for D17 score-formula context + observability metric aggregation per Codex #26 (cardinality control).

availability_world_hours.description: Per P1 D11 v2: world-clock hours when this good is available (e.g., [17,18,19,20,21,22] for evening-only pub_dinner). EMPTY array = always-available 24/7. needs_tick activity filters by current world-time hour before vendor_selection.

affinity_targets.description: Per P1 D11 v2 + D17 + Codex #9 fix (RENAMED from `affinity_categories` for clarity): list of party_ids (character_id or institution_id) the affinity weighting binds to. D17 score-formula: affinity_strength = max(char.affinities[].strength for any affinity whose target_id ∈ good.affinity_targets, else 0). Typical entry: the vendor institution_id (so chars who have positive affinity for Camden Arms Cafe preferentially buy from it).

capacity_per_world_day.description: Per P1 D11 v2 + Codex #19 fix: daily cap on how many units of this good can be sold per world-day. needs_tick activity tracks daily consumption per (good_id, world_date) via goods_daily_usage table; FCFS allocation per Codex #19 with locking/UPSERT to prevent overselling under parallel district ticks.

cost_basis_floor_multiplier.description: Per P0.5 v2 + Codex N3 tuning: minimum margin multiplier applied to cost_basis as price floor. price_adjustment activity uses floor = max(cost_basis_coin × cost_basis_floor_multiplier, old_price × 0.90). Defaults to 1.30 (30% minimum margin); founder content can tune per-good if needed.

version.description: Goods schema version. Bump when adding fields.
